name: create_find_similar_contacts_fn
statements:
create or replace function find_similar_contacts(input_name text, threshold float default 0.6)
returns table (id uuid, name text, company text, email text)
language sql
as $$
  select id, name, company, email
  from contacts
  where name is not null
    and similarity(name, input_name) >= threshold
  order by similarity(name, input_name) desc
  limit 5;
$$;

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_cron;

SELECT cron.schedule(
  'cleanup-orphan-cards',
  '0 3 * * *',
  $$
  DELETE FROM storage.objects
  WHERE bucket_id = 'cards'
    AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
    AND name NOT IN (
      SELECT substring(card_img_url from '.*/public/cards/(.+)$')
      FROM contacts WHERE card_img_url IS NOT NULL
      UNION
      SELECT substring(card_img_back_url from '.*/public/cards/(.+)$')
      FROM contacts WHERE card_img_back_url IS NOT NULL
      UNION
      SELECT storage_path FROM pending_contacts WHERE storage_path IS NOT NULL
    );
  $$
);

v1_7_duplicate_scan_functions
statements:
-- Find contacts with identical non-null email
create or replace function find_email_duplicates()
returns table(id_a uuid, id_b uuid)
language sql security definer as $$
  select a.id as id_a, b.id as id_b
  from contacts a
  join contacts b on a.email = b.email and a.id < b.id
  where a.email is not null and a.email <> '';
$$;

-- Find contacts with similar name (pg_trgm >= 0.6), excluding exact-email pairs
create or replace function find_name_duplicates()
returns table(id_a uuid, id_b uuid, score float)
language sql security definer as $$
  select a.id as id_a, b.id as id_b,
         greatest(
           similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
           similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
         ) as score
  from contacts a
  join contacts b on a.id < b.id
  where
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) >= 0.6
    -- exclude exact email pairs (already handled)
    and not (a.email is not null and a.email <> '' and a.email = b.email)
    -- exclude pairs where both names are empty
    and (a.name is not null or a.name_en is not null)
    and (b.name is not null or b.name_en is not null);
$$;

name: v1_7_find_similar_contact_by_name
statements:
-- Used by camcard import script to detect similar-name duplicates
create or replace function find_similar_contact_by_name(search_name text)
returns table(id uuid)
language sql security definer as $$
  select id from contacts
  where greatest(
    similarity(coalesce(name,''), search_name),
    similarity(coalesce(name_en,''), search_name)
  ) >= 0.6
  order by greatest(
    similarity(coalesce(name,''), search_name),
    similarity(coalesce(name_en,''), search_name)
  ) desc
  limit 1;
$$;

statements:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_interaction_logs_by_tags(
  p_tag_ids UUID[],
  p_date_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  p_date_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  p_created_by UUID DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  contact_name TEXT,
  contact_company TEXT,
  type TEXT,
  content TEXT,
  email_subject TEXT,
  meeting_date DATE,
  meeting_time TIME,
  meeting_location TEXT
) AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    c.name AS contact_name,
    c.company AS contact_company,
    il.type,
    il.content,
    il.email_subject,
    il.meeting_date,
    il.meeting_time::TIME,
    il.meeting_location
  FROM interaction_logs il
  JOIN contacts c ON c.id = il.contact_id
  WHERE il.type != 'system'
    AND (il.content IS NULL OR il.content NOT ILIKE '%透過 Telegram Bot 新增名片%')
    AND il.created_at >= p_date_from
    AND il.created_at <= p_date_to
    AND (
      p_tag_ids IS NULL
      OR cardinality(p_tag_ids) = 0
      OR il.contact_id IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT ct.contact_id FROM contact_tags ct WHERE ct.tag_id = ANY(p_tag_ids)
      )
    )
    AND (p_created_by IS NULL OR il.created_by = p_created_by)
  ORDER BY il.created_at DESC;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

name: get_users_mfa_status
statements:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_users_mfa_status()
RETURNS TABLE (email TEXT, has_mfa BOOLEAN)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$
  SELECT
    u.email,
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM auth.mfa_factors f
      WHERE f.user_id = u.id AND f.status = 'verified'
    ) AS has_mfa
  FROM auth.users u
  WHERE u.email IS NOT NULL;
$$;

name: get_interaction_logs_by_tags_v3_creator_drop_recreate
statements:
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_interaction_logs_by_tags(uuid[], timestamptz, timestamptz, uuid, text[], text[]);

CREATE FUNCTION get_interaction_logs_by_tags(
  p_tag_ids UUID[],
  p_date_from TIMESTAMPTZ,
  p_date_to TIMESTAMPTZ,
  p_created_by UUID DEFAULT NULL,
  p_country_codes TEXT[] DEFAULT NULL,
  p_types TEXT[] DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE (
  contact_name TEXT,
  contact_company TEXT,
  type TEXT,
  content TEXT,
  email_subject TEXT,
  meeting_date DATE,
  meeting_time TIME,
  meeting_location TEXT,
  creator_name TEXT
) AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    c.name AS contact_name,
    c.company AS contact_company,
    il.type,
    il.content,
    il.email_subject,
    il.meeting_date,
    il.meeting_time::TIME,
    il.meeting_location,
    u.display_name AS creator_name
  FROM interaction_logs il
  JOIN contacts c ON c.id = il.contact_id
  LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = il.created_by
  WHERE il.type != 'system'
    AND (il.content IS NULL OR il.content NOT ILIKE '%透過 Telegram Bot 新增名片%')
    AND il.created_at >= p_date_from
    AND il.created_at <= p_date_to
    AND (p_created_by IS NULL OR il.created_by = p_created_by)
    AND (
      p_tag_ids IS NULL OR cardinality(p_tag_ids) = 0
      OR il.contact_id IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT ct.contact_id FROM contact_tags ct WHERE ct.tag_id = ANY(p_tag_ids)
      )
    )
    AND (
      p_country_codes IS NULL OR cardinality(p_country_codes) = 0
      OR c.country_code = ANY(p_country_codes)
    )
    AND (
      p_types IS NULL OR cardinality(p_types) = 0
      OR il.type = ANY(p_types)
    )
  ORDER BY il.created_at DESC;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

name: fix_campaign_recipients_join_by_contact_id
statements:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_campaign_recipients(p_campaign_id uuid)
RETURNS TABLE(
  contact_id uuid,
  contact_name text,
  contact_email text,
  company text,
  delivered_at timestamp with time zone,
  first_opened_at timestamp with time zone,
  open_count bigint,
  last_clicked_at timestamp with time zone,
  bounced_at timestamp with time zone
)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
  SELECT
    c.id,
    c.name,
    c.email,
    c.company,
    MAX(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'delivered' THEN ee.occurred_at END),
    MIN(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'open'      THEN ee.occurred_at END),
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'open'    THEN 1 END),
    MAX(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'click'     THEN ee.occurred_at END),
    MAX(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'bounce'    THEN ee.occurred_at END)
  FROM interaction_logs il
  JOIN contacts c ON c.id = il.contact_id
  LEFT JOIN email_events ee
    ON ee.campaign_id = il.campaign_id
    AND (
      -- Prefer joining by contact_id (set by SendGrid custom_args)
      (ee.contact_id IS NOT NULL AND ee.contact_id = il.contact_id)
      OR
      -- Fallback: case-insensitive email match (for events without contact_id)
      (ee.contact_id IS NULL AND lower(ee.email) = lower(c.email))
    )
  WHERE il.campaign_id = p_campaign_id
    AND il.type = 'email'
  GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.email, c.company
  ORDER BY
    MIN(CASE WHEN ee.event = 'open' THEN ee.occurred_at END) ASC NULLS LAST,
    c.name;
$function$;

name: fix_duplicate_functions_filter_deleted
statements:
-- Fix find_email_duplicates: exclude deleted contacts
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_email_duplicates()
RETURNS TABLE(id_a uuid, id_b uuid)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
  SELECT a.id AS id_a, b.id AS id_b
  FROM contacts a
  JOIN contacts b ON lower(a.email) = lower(b.email) AND a.id < b.id
  WHERE a.email IS NOT NULL AND a.email <> ''
    AND a.deleted_at IS NULL
    AND b.deleted_at IS NULL;
$function$;

-- Fix find_name_duplicates: exclude deleted contacts + limit to 500 most recent to avoid timeout
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_name_duplicates()
RETURNS TABLE(id_a uuid, id_b uuid, score double precision)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
  WITH recent AS (
    SELECT id, name, name_en, email
    FROM contacts
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
    LIMIT 1000
  )
  SELECT a.id AS id_a, b.id AS id_b,
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) AS score
  FROM recent a
  JOIN recent b ON a.id < b.id
  WHERE
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) >= 0.6
    AND NOT (a.email IS NOT NULL AND a.email <> '' AND lower(a.email) = lower(b.email))
    AND (a.name IS NOT NULL OR a.name_en IS NOT NULL)
    AND (b.name IS NOT NULL OR b.name_en IS NOT NULL);
$function$;

-- ============================================================
-- 1. Helper functions for RLS policies
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.has_feature(feature_key text)
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, pg_temp
AS $$
  SELECT COALESCE(
    (SELECT CASE
      WHEN u.role = 'super_admin' THEN true
      ELSE u.granted_features @> ARRAY[feature_key]
    END
    FROM public.users u
    WHERE u.email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')),
    false
  )
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_super_admin()
RETURNS boolean
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, pg_temp
AS $$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.users u
    WHERE u.email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')
      AND u.role = 'super_admin'
  )
$$;

-- ============================================================
-- 2. ai_endpoints column privilege: hide api_key from all roles
--    except service_role (which is used by all API routes).
--    Enable RLS and allow authenticated to SELECT non-sensitive cols.
-- ============================================================

ALTER TABLE public.ai_endpoints ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "ai_endpoints_read" ON public.ai_endpoints;
CREATE POLICY "ai_endpoints_read" ON public.ai_endpoints
  FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "ai_endpoints_write" ON public.ai_endpoints;
CREATE POLICY "ai_endpoints_write" ON public.ai_endpoints
  FOR ALL TO authenticated
  USING (is_super_admin())
  WITH CHECK (is_super_admin());

-- Revoke column-level SELECT on api_key for anon/authenticated.
-- service_role keeps full access (it's a superuser-like role).
REVOKE SELECT ON public.ai_endpoints FROM anon, authenticated;
GRANT SELECT (id, name, base_url, is_active, created_at) ON public.ai_endpoints TO authenticated;
-- Writes go through service_role or fall under the RLS super_admin policy; authenticated
-- cannot INSERT/UPDATE api_key directly because the column is not granted. But the
-- super_admin UI does write it via /admin/models. That UI currently uses browser client;
-- if the admin is super_admin they hit the RLS policy, but without INSERT column grant
-- the write still fails. So we GRANT INSERT/UPDATE on all columns; RLS policy still gates
-- on is_super_admin().
GRANT INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON public.ai_endpoints TO authenticated;

-- ============================================================
-- Storage bucket policies hardening
--
-- Buckets stay public=true so direct object URLs keep working
-- (e.g. https://.../storage/v1/object/public/cards/file.jpg).
-- We tighten the SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies on
-- storage.objects so anonymous users can't LIST or upload.
-- ============================================================

-- Remove broad "anyone can list cards" policy
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Public read cards" ON storage.objects;

-- Remove previous broad "anyone can upload to cards" policy
-- (it was labelled "Service role upload cards" but had role=public)
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Service role upload cards" ON storage.objects;

-- Cards bucket: authenticated users can read/upload/update/delete their org's card images
-- (bot uses service_role and bypasses RLS anyway)
CREATE POLICY "cards_authenticated_select"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'cards');

CREATE POLICY "cards_authenticated_insert"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'cards');

CREATE POLICY "cards_authenticated_update"
  ON storage.objects FOR UPDATE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'cards')
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'cards');

CREATE POLICY "cards_authenticated_delete"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'cards');

-- camcard bucket: same pattern for consistency
CREATE POLICY "camcard_authenticated_select"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'camcard');

CREATE POLICY "camcard_authenticated_insert"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'camcard');

CREATE POLICY "camcard_authenticated_update"
  ON storage.objects FOR UPDATE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'camcard')
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'camcard');

CREATE POLICY "camcard_authenticated_delete"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'camcard');

-- template-attachments: authenticated for read+write
CREATE POLICY "template_attachments_authenticated_select"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'template-attachments');

CREATE POLICY "template_attachments_authenticated_insert"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT TO authenticated
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'template-attachments');

CREATE POLICY "template_attachments_authenticated_update"
  ON storage.objects FOR UPDATE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'template-attachments')
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'template-attachments');

CREATE POLICY "template_attachments_authenticated_delete"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'template-attachments');

-- feedback bucket: the existing "auth users can upload own feedback screenshots"
-- policy covers INSERT. Add SELECT/DELETE for completeness (authenticated)
CREATE POLICY "feedback_authenticated_select"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  USING (bucket_id = 'feedback');

CREATE POLICY "feedback_authenticated_delete"
  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE TO authenticated

statements:
-- Exclude newsletter campaign sends from last_activity_at calculation.
-- Rationale: Po noticed that bulk sending a newsletter to 4000+ subscribers
-- would stamp every contact's last_activity_at to "now", wiping out the
-- "who did I recently talk to" signal.
--
-- Heuristic: interaction_logs.campaign_id IS NOT NULL → newsletter send → skip.
-- Individual 1-to-1 email (contact detail page, bot, etc.) has campaign_id = NULL.
-- Ad-hoc bulk compose without campaign_id still counts — that's intentional,
-- user bulk emailing 10 people DOES want to follow up with them.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_contact_last_activity()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$
DECLARE
  target_contact_id uuid;
BEGIN
  target_contact_id := COALESCE(NEW.contact_id, OLD.contact_id);
  IF target_contact_id IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NULL;
  END IF;

  UPDATE contacts
  SET last_activity_at = GREATEST(
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT MAX(il.created_at) FROM interaction_logs il
       WHERE il.contact_id = target_contact_id
         AND il.type IN ('note','meeting','email')
         AND il.campaign_id IS NULL),
      created_at
    ),
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT MAX(cp.created_at) FROM contact_photos cp
       WHERE cp.contact_id = target_contact_id),
      created_at
    )
  )
  WHERE id = target_contact_id;

  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.contact_id IS NOT NULL AND OLD.contact_id <> NEW.contact_id THEN
    UPDATE contacts
    SET last_activity_at = GREATEST(
      COALESCE(
        (SELECT MAX(il.created_at) FROM interaction_logs il
         WHERE il.contact_id = OLD.contact_id
           AND il.type IN ('note','meeting','email')
           AND il.campaign_id IS NULL),
        created_at
      ),
      COALESCE(
        (SELECT MAX(cp.created_at) FROM contact_photos cp
         WHERE cp.contact_id = OLD.contact_id),
        created_at
      )
    )
    WHERE id = OLD.contact_id;
  END IF;

  RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_contact_last_activity_from_photo()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$
DECLARE
  target_contact_id uuid;
BEGIN
  target_contact_id := COALESCE(NEW.contact_id, OLD.contact_id);
  IF target_contact_id IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NULL;
  END IF;

  UPDATE contacts
  SET last_activity_at = GREATEST(
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT MAX(il.created_at) FROM interaction_logs il
       WHERE il.contact_id = target_contact_id
         AND il.type IN ('note','meeting','email')
         AND il.campaign_id IS NULL),
      created_at
    ),
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT MAX(cp.created_at) FROM contact_photos cp
       WHERE cp.contact_id = target_contact_id),
      created_at
    )
  )
  WHERE id = target_contact_id;

  RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;

-- Backfill: recompute everyone with the new filter (newsletter logs now excluded)
UPDATE contacts c
SET last_activity_at = GREATEST(
  COALESCE(

     WHERE il.contact_id = c.id

-- Add send_method to interaction_logs so last_activity can exclude ALL SendGrid
-- sends (not just campaign-tagged ones). SendGrid is used both for newsletter
-- campaigns AND ad-hoc bulk compose — both should not count as "I recently
-- talked to this person" because it's automated mass comms.

  ADD COLUMN send_method text
  CHECK (send_method IS NULL OR send_method IN ('outlook', 'sendgrid'));

name: reverse_link_subscriber_to_contact
statements:
-- Add reverse-direction trigger: when a subscriber is inserted or email
-- updated, auto-link to a matching contact if one exists. Complements the
-- existing forward trigger link_subscribers_on_contact_email (fires on
-- contacts INSERT/UPDATE of email).

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION link_subscriber_from_contact_on_subscriber()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
BEGIN
  IF NEW.email IS NULL OR NEW.contact_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  UPDATE newsletter_subscribers s
  SET contact_id = c.id
  FROM contacts c
  WHERE s.id = NEW.id
    AND c.deleted_at IS NULL
    AND c.email IS NOT NULL
    AND lower(c.email) = lower(NEW.email);

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_link_subscriber_on_insert ON newsletter_subscribers;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_link_subscriber_on_insert
  AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OF email ON newsletter_subscribers
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION link_subscriber_from_contact_on_subscriber();

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION dashboard_email_status_stats()
RETURNS TABLE(category text, count bigint) AS $$
  SELECT
    COALESCE(email_status, 'ok')::text AS category,
    COUNT(*)::bigint AS count
  FROM contacts
  WHERE email IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
  GROUP BY 1

  UNION ALL

  SELECT 'external'::text AS category, COUNT(DISTINCT all_emails.email)::bigint
  FROM (
    SELECT LOWER(TRIM(email))::text AS email FROM newsletter_subscribers WHERE email IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT email::text FROM newsletter_blacklist
    UNION
    SELECT email::text FROM newsletter_unsubscribes
  ) all_emails

    WHERE LOWER(TRIM(c.email)) = all_emails.email AND c.deleted_at IS NULL

$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE;

name: unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete
statements:
-- When a contact is soft-deleted, auto-unsubscribe matching newsletter subscribers
-- and add a global unsubscribe record so they won't be sent newsletters again.
-- Restoring a contact does NOT auto re-subscribe — that has to be manual.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete()
RETURNS trigger
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $$
DECLARE
  norm_email text;
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when transitioning to deleted (was NULL, now NOT NULL)
  IF OLD.deleted_at IS NOT NULL OR NEW.deleted_at IS NULL OR NEW.email IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  norm_email := LOWER(TRIM(NEW.email));

  -- 1. Mark related subscribers as unsubscribed
  UPDATE newsletter_subscribers
  SET unsubscribed_at = NEW.deleted_at
  WHERE LOWER(TRIM(email::text)) = norm_email
    AND unsubscribed_at IS NULL;

  -- 2. Add to global unsubscribe list (idempotent)
  INSERT INTO newsletter_unsubscribes (email, contact_id, source, reason, unsubscribed_at)
  VALUES (
    norm_email,
    NEW.id,
    'contact_soft_delete',
    '聯絡人軟刪除自動退訂（' || COALESCE(NEW.name, NEW.name_en, NEW.name_local, '無姓名') || '）',
    NEW.deleted_at
  )
  ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE
  SET unsubscribed_at = EXCLUDED.unsubscribed_at,
      source = EXCLUDED.source,
      reason = EXCLUDED.reason;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trg_unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete ON contacts;
CREATE TRIGGER trg_unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete
  AFTER UPDATE OF deleted_at ON contacts
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete();

name: dashboard_external_excludes_unsubs
statements:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION dashboard_email_status_stats()
RETURNS TABLE(category text, count bigint) AS $$
  -- CRM contacts (with email)
  SELECT
    COALESCE(email_status, 'ok')::text AS category,
    COUNT(*)::bigint AS count
  FROM contacts
  WHERE email IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
  GROUP BY 1

  UNION ALL

  -- External emails: in subscribers but NOT in active CRM
  -- AND not unsubscribed (subscribers.unsubscribed_at + newsletter_unsubscribes)
  -- AND not blacklisted
  SELECT 'external'::text, COUNT(DISTINCT s_email.email)::bigint
  FROM (
    SELECT LOWER(TRIM(email::text)) AS email
    FROM newsletter_subscribers
    WHERE email IS NOT NULL
      AND unsubscribed_at IS NULL
  ) s_email
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM contacts c
    WHERE LOWER(TRIM(c.email)) = s_email.email AND c.deleted_at IS NULL
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM newsletter_unsubscribes u WHERE u.email = s_email.email
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM newsletter_blacklist b WHERE b.email = s_email.email
  );
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE;

-- 1. pending_contacts: add async worker fields
ALTER TABLE pending_contacts
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'done',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS retry_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS error_message TEXT;

ALTER TABLE pending_contacts ALTER COLUMN data SET DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;

ALTER TABLE pending_contacts
  ADD CONSTRAINT pending_contacts_status_check
  CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'processing', 'done', 'failed'));

-- Cron worker query index
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS pending_contacts_status_created_idx
  ON pending_contacts (status, created_at)
  WHERE status IN ('pending', 'processing');

-- User pending list query index
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS pending_contacts_user_status_idx
  ON pending_contacts (created_by, status, created_at DESC);

-- 2. pending_contacts RLS: replace wide-open with per-user + super_admin
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS pending_contacts_all ON pending_contacts;

CREATE POLICY pending_contacts_own_all ON pending_contacts FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (created_by = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')))
  WITH CHECK (created_by = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')));

CREATE POLICY pending_contacts_super_admin_all ON pending_contacts FOR ALL
  TO public
  USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email') AND role = 'super_admin'))
  WITH CHECK (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email') AND role = 'super_admin'));

-- 3. failed_scans RLS: tighten read from wide-open to per-user, add per-user write
DROP POLICY IF EXISTS failed_scans_read ON failed_scans;

CREATE POLICY failed_scans_own_all ON failed_scans FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')))
  WITH CHECK (user_id = (SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = (auth.jwt() ->> 'email')));
-- existing: failed_scans_write (has_feature 'failed_scans') and super_admin_all stay

name: fix_unsubscribe_trigger_skip_when_other_active_contact_exists
statements:
-- 修 trigger：軟刪除聯絡人時，若同 email 還有「活著的」聯絡人，不要退訂。
-- 原因：去重合併聯絡人時，敗北方被軟刪除，但同 email 的勝出方還活著且想繼續收信。
-- 之前的 trigger 沒檢查這個，結果整個 email 一起退訂，連勝出方也收不到信。

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.unsubscribe_on_contact_soft_delete()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
DECLARE
  norm_email text;
  has_other_active boolean;
BEGIN
  -- Only fire when transitioning to deleted (was NULL, now NOT NULL)
  IF OLD.deleted_at IS NOT NULL OR NEW.deleted_at IS NULL OR NEW.email IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  norm_email := LOWER(TRIM(NEW.email));

  -- Guard: skip when another non-deleted contact still owns this email.
  -- This is the dedup-merge case — the surviving contact still wants the newsletter.
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM contacts
    WHERE LOWER(TRIM(email)) = norm_email
      AND id != NEW.id
      AND deleted_at IS NULL
  ) INTO has_other_active;

  IF has_other_active THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  -- 1. Mark related subscribers as unsubscribed
  UPDATE newsletter_subscribers
  SET unsubscribed_at = NEW.deleted_at
  WHERE LOWER(TRIM(email::text)) = norm_email
    AND unsubscribed_at IS NULL;

  -- 2. Add to global unsubscribe list (idempotent)
  INSERT INTO newsletter_unsubscribes (email, contact_id, source, reason, unsubscribed_at)
  VALUES (
    norm_email,
    NEW.id,
    'contact_soft_delete',
    '聯絡人軟刪除自動退訂（' || COALESCE(NEW.name, NEW.name_en, NEW.name_local, '無姓名') || '）',
    NEW.deleted_at
  )
  ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE
  SET unsubscribed_at = EXCLUDED.unsubscribed_at,
      source = EXCLUDED.source,
      reason = EXCLUDED.reason;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$;

-- 回復這 77 筆誤判：清掉 backfill 那批的退訂紀錄 + 對應的 subscribers
WITH backfill_targets AS (
  SELECT u.email, c.deleted_at
  FROM newsletter_unsubscribes u
  LEFT JOIN contacts c ON c.id = u.contact_id
  WHERE u.source = 'contact_soft_delete_backfill'
),
sub_restore AS (
  UPDATE newsletter_subscribers s
  SET unsubscribed_at = NULL
  FROM backfill_targets b
  WHERE LOWER(TRIM(s.email::text)) = b.email
    AND s.unsubscribed_at = b.deleted_at
  RETURNING s.id
)
DELETE FROM newsletter_unsubscribes
WHERE source = 'contact_soft_delete_backfill';

name: restore_users_select_grant
statements:
-- users 表 anon/authenticated 莫名其妙沒有 SELECT GRANT，導致任何 RLS policy
-- 內 EXISTS (SELECT FROM users ...) 子查詢都 permission denied、整個母 query
-- 失敗。今天 /admin/newsletter/campaigns 顯示 empty 就是這樣壞的：browser
-- 用 anon key 查 newsletter_campaigns，policy super_admin full access 內部
-- 要查 users，permission 被拒、整個 query 回 error。
--
-- RLS users_select policy 是 qual=true 開放讀，所以恢復 GRANT 不增加實際
-- 暴露面：原本就應該允許 read。

GRANT SELECT ON public.users TO anon, authenticated;

fix_duplicate_scan_exact_name_and_remove_limit
statements:
-- 1. New function: exact canonical name match, full table, O(n log n)
-- Canonical = COALESCE(name, name_en), trimmed. No LIMIT, no trigram needed.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_exact_name_duplicates()
RETURNS TABLE(id_a uuid, id_b uuid)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public', 'pg_temp'
AS $$
  WITH named AS (
    SELECT
      id,
      COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(name_en), '')) AS canonical
    FROM contacts
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
      AND COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(name), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(name_en), '')) IS NOT NULL
  )
  SELECT a.id AS id_a, b.id AS id_b
  FROM named a
  JOIN named b ON a.canonical = b.canonical AND a.id < b.id;
$$;

-- 2. Replace find_name_duplicates: remove LIMIT 1000, scan full table
--    Raise threshold slightly to 0.65 to reduce noise at larger scale.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_name_duplicates()
RETURNS TABLE(id_a uuid, id_b uuid, score double precision)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public', 'pg_temp'
AS $$
  SELECT a.id AS id_a, b.id AS id_b,
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) AS score
  FROM contacts a
  JOIN contacts b ON a.id < b.id
  WHERE a.deleted_at IS NULL
    AND b.deleted_at IS NULL
    AND greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) >= 0.65
    AND NOT (a.email IS NOT NULL AND a.email <> '' AND lower(a.email) = lower(b.email))
    AND (a.name IS NOT NULL OR a.name_en IS NOT NULL)
    AND (b.name IS NOT NULL OR b.name_en IS NOT NULL)
    -- Exclude exact name matches (already handled by find_exact_name_duplicates)
    AND NOT (
      COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.name),''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.name_en),''))
      = COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(b.name),''), NULLIF(TRIM(b.name_en),''))
    );
$$;

name: fix_name_duplicates_restore_limit
statements:
-- find_exact_name_duplicates handles all exact matches (fast, full table).
-- find_name_duplicates only needs fuzzy/typo cases — restore LIMIT to avoid O(n²) timeout.
-- Raised to 2000 (from original 1000) to cover more recent contacts.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_name_duplicates()
RETURNS TABLE(id_a uuid, id_b uuid, score double precision)
LANGUAGE sql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public', 'pg_temp'
AS $$
  WITH recent AS (
    SELECT id, name, name_en, email
    FROM contacts
    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
    LIMIT 2000
  )
  SELECT a.id AS id_a, b.id AS id_b,
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) AS score
  FROM recent a
  JOIN recent b ON a.id < b.id
  WHERE
    greatest(
      similarity(coalesce(a.name,''), coalesce(b.name,'')),
      similarity(coalesce(a.name_en,''), coalesce(b.name_en,''))
    ) >= 0.65
    AND NOT (a.email IS NOT NULL AND a.email <> '' AND lower(a.email) = lower(b.email))
    AND (a.name IS NOT NULL OR a.name_en IS NOT NULL)
    AND (b.name IS NOT NULL OR b.name_en IS NOT NULL)
    -- Exclude exact name matches (handled by find_exact_name_duplicates)
    AND NOT (
      COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.name),''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.name_en),''))
      = COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(b.name),''), NULLIF(TRIM(b.name_en),''))
    );
$$;

name: add_creator_ids_filter_to_interaction_logs_rpc
statements:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_interaction_logs_by_tags(
  p_tag_ids uuid[],
  p_date_from timestamp with time zone,
  p_date_to timestamp with time zone,
  p_created_by uuid DEFAULT NULL,
  p_country_codes text[] DEFAULT NULL,
  p_types text[] DEFAULT NULL,
  p_created_by_ids uuid[] DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE(
  contact_name text,
  contact_company text,
  type text,
  content text,
  email_subject text,
  meeting_date date,
  meeting_time time without time zone,
  meeting_location text,
  creator_name text
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path TO 'public', 'pg_temp'
AS $$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT
    c.name AS contact_name,
    c.company AS contact_company,
    il.type,
    il.content,
    il.email_subject,
    il.meeting_date,
    il.meeting_time::TIME,
    il.meeting_location,
    u.display_name AS creator_name
  FROM interaction_logs il
  JOIN contacts c ON c.id = il.contact_id
  LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = il.created_by
  WHERE il.type != 'system'
    AND (il.content IS NULL OR il.content NOT ILIKE '%透過 Telegram Bot 新增名片%')
    AND il.created_at >= p_date_from
    AND il.created_at <= p_date_to
    AND (p_created_by IS NULL OR il.created_by = p_created_by)
    AND (
      p_created_by_ids IS NULL
      OR cardinality(p_created_by_ids) = 0
      OR il.created_by = ANY(p_created_by_ids)
    )
    AND (
      p_tag_ids IS NULL OR cardinality(p_tag_ids) = 0
      OR il.contact_id IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT ct.contact_id FROM contact_tags ct WHERE ct.tag_id = ANY(p_tag_ids)
      )
    )
    AND (
      p_country_codes IS NULL OR cardinality(p_country_codes) = 0
      OR c.country_code = ANY(p_country_codes)
    )
    AND (
      p_types IS NULL OR cardinality(p_types) = 0
      OR il.type = ANY(p_types)
    )
  ORDER BY il.created_at DESC;
END;
$$;
